title: elaphoglossum crinitum
intro: Elaphoglossum crinitum is a species of fern in the family Dryopteridaceae, commonly known as the hairy elaphoglossum or hairy lycopodium fern. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of Elaphoglossum crinitum include its ability to reduce fever, treat respiratory issues, and alleviate symptoms of skin conditions such as eczema and acne. Herbal preparations of Elaphoglossum crinitum can be made by infusing the leaves in hot water to create a tea, or by using the plant in a topical cream or ointment to soothe and calm the skin.
